title: De institutione inclusarum (Vernon translation)
summary: The repository artifact is the CME-submodule XML for University of Michigan item CME00003. U-M encoded Carl Horstmann's first edition in Englische Studien 7 (1884), pp. 304-344: a German introduction, the nineteen-chapter Middle English Vernon translation, and an older parallel Latin text. Horstmann transcribed the Middle English work from the Vernon Manuscript, now Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, where it occupies fols. IIIv-VIIIr (official IIIF canvases 10-19); his legacy page letters a-k refer to the same ten manuscript pages. The translation, attributed in its medieval rubric to Thomas N., renders only the final twenty sections of Aelred of Rievaulx's De institutione inclusarum. A complete 710-canvas color surrogate of the physical Vernon codex and a complete 524-canvas scan of the 1884 journal volume have been cached as auditable IIIF bundles, with the work mapped inside each full object. Ayto's 1973 manuscript-based thesis is openly downloadable; the 1984 Ayto-Barratt EETS edition is represented as a later comparison source available through purchase and controlled borrowing, not as an ancestor of the U-M text.
review_notes: The cataloged work is the partial Middle English Vernon translation, not Aelred's complete Latin treatise, Horstmann's German introduction or parallel Latin text, the distinct Bodley 423 translation, or the whole Vernon miscellany.
The official Bodleian IIIF range fixes the work at modern fols. IIIv-VIIIr and canvases 10-19; visual inspection confirms the opening rubric and final Amen.

The complete 710-canvas physical codex was cached rather than only the ten target canvases. The bundle's 1600-pixel image requests preserve every native-size fallback URL in its inventory; complete means complete surface coverage, not preservation-master resolution.
Horstmann's full journal volume was likewise cached as a complete 524-canvas bundle; his article occupies printed pp. 304-344, IIIF canvases 320-360.
Bodleian image licensing, BSB scan terms, the medieval text, old and modern editions, U-M encoding, Durham thesis, and local XML are separate rights layers.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00003
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00003
entities.description: A publicly viewable electronic text of Horstmann's article. It preserves the German introduction, serializes the Middle English translation and parallel Latin text as separate divisions, and retains printed page milestones, editorial notes, italics, and superscript expansions.
notes: The compact wrapper exposes no item-level statement identifying OCR versus manual keying or an original electronic-publication date.
A revision note dated 6-2-16 records structural validation, title-page proofing, page-break review, and conversion of the Latin comparison from notes into a version DIV1 with chapter milestones.
The 81 PB elements repeat print pages across the introduction, Middle English, and Latin divisions and do not represent 81 distinct manuscript surfaces.

entities.label: Horstmann's 1884 edition of the Vernon translation
alternate_labels: Informacio Alredi abbatis monasterij de Rieualle ad sororem suam inclusam: translata de latino in anglicum per Thomam N.
entities.description: The immediate print source for U-M: Horstmann's German introduction and Middle English transcription from Vernon fols. a-k, accompanied page by page by the Latin text then printed under pseudo-Augustine in Patrologia Latina 32. Horstmann notes uncertain abbreviation expansions and the different English and Latin chapter systems.

date_statements.label: publication
date_statements.notes: Horstmann's signature reads Berlin, October 1883. The issue 1 title page is dated 1883, while the completed-volume title page and standard article citations are dated 1884.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Informacio Alredi abbatis monasterij de Rieualle ad sororem suam inclusam: translata de latino in anglicum per Thomam N.,' Englische Studien 7 (1884), 304-344.
bibliographic.title: Informacio Alredi abbatis monasterij de Rieualle ad sororem suam inclusam: translata de latino in anglicum per Thomam N.

notes: The scan's issue 1 title page reads Heilbronn: Verlag von Gebr. Henninger, 1883, and its completed-volume title page repeats that place and publisher with the date 1884. BSB/DDB catalog metadata instead gives Leipzig: O. R. Reisland, 1884; the bibliographic fields follow the direct title-page evidence.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. a. 1
entities.description: The sole known witness of this Middle English translation. It occupies ten pages in the manuscript's added preliminary quire, modern fols. IIIv-VIIIr; Horstmann's later page lettering calls the same span fols. a-k.

date_statements.label: preliminary-quire assessment
date_statements.value: about 1390, possibly extending into the early fifteenth century
date_statements.notes: Ayto's older palaeographical discussion is retained separately from the current Bodleian whole-codex date.

holding.notes: Digital Bodleian supplies the exact item record and a complete 710-canvas surrogate.

physical_description.extent: viii + 342 + i leaves, incomplete; originally viii + 413, with 71 leaves thought lost. The work occupies fols. IIIv-VIIIr.
physical_description.format: Large religious and didactic miscellany, normally in gatherings of eight
notes: The codex contains more than 350 items and is written in double and triple columns; the work's preliminary-quire pages use three columns.
The current item description gives England, West Midlands? as origin and 1381-1400 as its date statement.
Ayto describes the work quire's hand as Anglicana Formata and its surface language as localized between Worcester and Birmingham.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: A complete color digital surrogate of the physical codex, including covers, flyleaves, all surviving manuscript leaves, and the ten pages carrying the cataloged work.
entities.reproduction_format: 710 color IIIF canvases; locally bundled as 1600-pixel-wide JPEG requests plus manifest and exact-source inventory
notes: The cached bundle covers the complete Digital Bodleian physical object, not only fols. IIIv-VIIIr.
The internal inventory preserves canvas labels, canvas URLs, exact image requests, effective request profiles, native full-size fallback URLs, member sizes, and checksums.

entities.label: BSB digital facsimile of Englische Studien 7 (1884)
entities.description: A complete 524-canvas scan of BSB copy P.o.angl. 413 h-7. Horstmann's article occupies printed pp. 304-344, digital canvases 320-360.
entities.reproduction_format: 524 IIIF JPEG canvases plus manifest; complete local IIIF ZIP bundle

entities.label: Aelred of Rievaulx, De institutione inclusarum
entities.description: Aelred's Latin rule for his enclosed sister. The Vernon translation renders only its final twenty sections, which concern inner spiritual life, personal morality, prayer, and meditation; it omits the opening practical directions for external anchoritic observance.
notes: This entity represents the source work, not a claim that Thomas N. used any identified surviving Latin manuscript or printed edition.

entities.label: Migne, Patrologia Latina 32 text of De vita eremitica ad sororem
entities.description: The older pseudo-Augustinian Latin text at PL 32, cols. 1451-1474, which Horstmann printed beneath his Middle English transcription for comparison. Ayto notes that this Latin version is unreliable and lacks most of the passage corresponding to English chapter 18.

bibliographic.citation: Patrologiae cursus completus, Series Latina, vol. 32 (Paris: J.-P. Migne, 1857), cols. 1451-1474.
bibliographic.title: Patrologiae cursus completus, Series Latina, volume 32

entities.label: Ayto's 1973 edition and study of the Vernon translation
entities.description: An openly available 430-page Durham MA thesis containing a new manuscript-based edition beside C. H. Talbot's Latin text, extensive introduction, commentary, an appendix listing disagreements with Horstmann, and a glossary.

bibliographic.citation: J. R. Ayto, 'An edition (with commentary, a full glossary and an introduction on the language and the literary interest of the text) of the Middle English version of Aelred of Rievaulx's De Institutione Inclusarum in the Vernon MS' (MA thesis, Durham University, 1973).
bibliographic.title: An edition of the Middle English version of Aelred of Rievaulx's De Institutione Inclusarum in the Vernon MS

entities.label: Ayto and Barratt's De institutione inclusarum: Two English Versions
entities.description: The modern EETS edition of both Middle English translations, including the partial Vernon version and the distinct fuller Bodley 423 version. It is a later comparison source, not the edition used by U-M.

bibliographic.citation: Aelred of Rievaulx's De institutione inclusarum: Two English Versions, ed. John Ayto and Alexandra Barratt, Early English Text Society, Original Series 287 (London and New York: Oxford University Press for EETS, 1984).
bibliographic.title: Aelred of Rievaulx's De institutione inclusarum: Two English Versions
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 287
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: The April 2026 revision of U.S. Copyright Office duration guidance, used only for this manifest's United States assessment of the 1884 and 1857 editions; it is not part of the textual lineage.

assertion.notes: The identifiers, title, page structure, and full text align; no independently downloadable current U-M source XML was located for a byte-level comparison.

rights.component: The medieval Middle English translation and Aelred's underlying Latin work
rights.basis: The source work and translation are medieval; no copyright term persists in their historical wording. This does not determine rights in modern editions, encodings, or photographs.

rights.component: Horstmann's 1884 transcription, introduction, notes, and the incorporated older Migne comparison text
rights.basis: The 1884 article and 1857 Migne volume are far older than the 95-year maximum U.S. term described for pre-1978 protected works; U-M also labels its Horstmann-derived item public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item CME00003
rights.basis: The current item page expressly states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and is not a worldwide license or dedication.

rights.component: The local XML serialization and any repository-specific changes
rights.jurisdiction: Repository terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: U-M labels the upstream item public domain in the United States, but the local XML supplies no express license governing this serialization or later repository changes.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian manuscript photographs and cached IIIF JPEGs
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and CC BY-NC 4.0 license terms
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest attributes the photographs to Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0; Digital Bodleian terms require attribution and prohibit commercial reuse absent permission.
notes: Local caching does not grant permission to publish the bundle or images commercially.

rights.component: BSB digital images of its 1884 journal copy and cached IIIF JPEGs
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The BSB IIIF manifest and DDB item apply No Copyright-Non-Commercial Use Only to the digital object.
notes: The provider's noncommercial condition concerns BSB's digital surrogate; it does not alter the U.S. public-domain status of the underlying 1884 article.

rights.component: Ayto's 1973 thesis text, edition, commentary, and glossary
rights.jurisdiction: Durham e-Theses provider terms and applicable copyright law
rights.basis: The downloaded PDF's Durham use statement permits unchanged reproduction with full citation and a metadata-record link for personal research, study, educational, or not-for-profit purposes, but says the full text must not be sold without formal permission from the copyright holders.

rights.component: The 1984 editorial text, introduction, notes, and apparatus
rights.jurisdiction: United States, United Kingdom, and provider terms
rights.basis: The scholarly edition was published in 1984 and no open license or public-domain assertion was found. Its Internet Archive copy is access-restricted and offered through controlled borrowing rather than anonymous download.
notes: The medieval text remains public domain, but copying the modern edition's editorial matter or transcription can raise separate rights questions.

rights.component: Internet Archive / Google Books scan derivative of the 1857 volume
rights.basis: The underlying 1857 edition is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific open license governing the scan derivative was verified.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript suspension marks and abbreviation strokes
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters are printed in italics; Horstmann expressly flags uncertainty over the loop read as is/es and whether barred -on should be expanded -oun or -one.
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann explains that he expands the stroke on forms such as ell and gell as is (possibly es) and normally expands barred -on as -oun, while acknowledging alternate manuscript forms.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Different chapter systems in the nineteen-chapter English translation and the older seventy-eight-chapter Latin text
editorial_practices.representation: A corresponding Migne Latin passage is printed beneath the Middle English on each page; Latin chapter headings are omitted, and cross-references identify English and Latin chapter numbers.
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann presents the Latin as comparison material and comments on its defective transmission, including the long omission supplied by the English chapter 18; this is not evidence that the medieval translator used Migne.
editorial_practices.scope: Parallel Latin text and introduction, pp. 304-344

editorial_practices.source_feature: Horstmann's page-by-page English text with Latin comparison at the foot of each printed page
editorial_practices.representation: The XML keeps the German introduction first, then serializes the complete Middle English version as one DIV1 and the parallel Latin as a later DIV1, repeating PB milestones for their common printed pages.
editorial_practices.description: This structural transformation makes the Latin look like a subsequent version rather than a page-level parallel, so consumers should not infer work order from DIV1 order alone.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Red manuscript title, chapter headings, subheadings, and colored or illuminated initials as reproduced typographically by Horstmann
editorial_practices.representation: Titles and chapter labels are HEAD elements; the encoding does not describe manuscript color, decoration, or all physical initials, while HI italic preserves Horstmann's typographic distinctions.
editorial_practices.description: The XML represents the printed edition's headings and emphasis, not a diplomatic color-and-layout model of the Vernon pages.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Vernon manuscript readings, Horstmann's earlier edition, and C. H. Talbot's modern Latin text
editorial_practices.representation: A new Middle English text is printed beside Talbot's Latin; emendations are marked, commentary explains readings, and an appendix lists Horstmann's divergences from the manuscript.
editorial_practices.description: Ayto's edition is an independent later manuscript-based comparison source and helps diagnose Horstmann's transcription; it is not the source of the U-M encoding.
editorial_practices.scope: Thesis text pp. 90-208, commentary, and Appendix p. 263

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME00003.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00003 and preserves Horstmann's title, German introduction, nineteen-chapter English translation, separately serialized Latin comparison, pagination, notes, italic expansions, and 2016 structural-review record.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item CME00003.
evidence.locator: item information, rights, and full text
evidence.summary: Current item page identifies the work, Aelred and Horstmann, Englische Studien 7 (1884), the U-M digital text, and a public-domain / No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Informacio Alredi abbatis monasterij de Rieualle ad sororem suam inclusam: translata de latino in anglicum per Thomam N.,' Englische Studien 7 (1884), 304-344.
evidence.locator: pp. 304-305 introduction; pp. 305-344 Middle English and Latin text; p. 344 signature
evidence.summary: Direct inspection establishes title, editor, Vernon fols. a-k, Thomas N. attribution, partial relationship to Aelred's work, use of the Migne Latin, nineteen English chapters, abbreviation-expansion conventions, and the Berlin October 1883 signature in the 1884 volume.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek / Münchener DigitalisierungsZentrum, Englische Studien 7 (1884), BSB-ID 991030196999707356.
evidence.locator: issue 1 title page at canvas 5; completed-volume title page at canvas 9; catalog and manifest metadata; article at canvases 320-360
evidence.summary: Direct inspection gives Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1883 on the issue 1 title page and Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1884 on the completed-volume title page; BSB/DDB catalog metadata instead gives Leipzig: Reisland 1884. The record also confirms BSB shelfmark P.o.angl. 413 h-7, the 524-canvas scan, Horstmann article mapping, IIIF access, and the No Copyright-Non-Commercial rights URI.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript), Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest and range 'De Institutione Inclusarum'.
evidence.locator: item metadata; IIIF range LOG_0003; fols. IIIv-VIIIr; canvases 10-19
evidence.summary: Official metadata gives shelfmark, 1381-1400 date, West Midlands? origin, physical extent, incomplete state, 710-canvas full surrogate, exact work folios and canvases, Aelred attribution, and CC BY-NC image terms.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian images of MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, fols. IIIv and VIIIr.
evidence.summary: The opening canvas visibly carries the red rubric naming Aelred and Thomas N.; the final canvas contains the close and Amen, corroborating the official work boundary.

evidence.locator: Terms of use; permitted use; prohibited use
evidence.summary: Explains CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution requirements, permits noncommercial research and specified academic uses, and requires permission and fees for commercial uses; the exact item manifest applies that license to the Vernon photographs.

evidence.citation: J. R. Ayto, 'An edition ... of the Middle English version of Aelred of Rievaulx's De Institutione Inclusarum in the Vernon MS' (MA thesis, Durham University, 1973).
evidence.locator: Introduction pp. 1-18, 30-35, 80-86; text pp. 90-208; Appendix p. 263
evidence.summary: Provides a direct manuscript-based edition and detailed evidence for folios, work scope, two-part structure, translator uncertainty, approximate chronology, stratified dialect, Horstmann variants, and Talbot Latin correspondence.

evidence.citation: Durham e-Theses use statement embedded in Ayto's downloaded thesis and metadata record.
evidence.summary: Permits unchanged full-text reproduction with bibliographic reference and metadata link for personal research, study, education, or not-for-profit purposes; prohibits sale without copyright-holder permission.

evidence.citation: Aelred of Rievaulx's De institutione inclusarum: Two English Versions, ed. John Ayto and Alexandra Barratt, EETS OS 287 (Oxford University Press, 1984), ISBN 9780197222898.
evidence.summary: Confirms Ayto and Barratt's 1984 EETS edition, series and ISBN, Middle English and Latin contents, and transcription from the Vernon Manuscript and Bodley 423.

evidence.citation: Early English Text Society, List of All Publications (2023).
evidence.summary: Lists Ayto and Barratt's edition, 1984 date, ISBN 9780197222898, and a 2023 price of £30 / US$50, with Oxford University Press ordering information.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item aelredofrievaulx0000aelr, Ayto and Barratt 1984 EETS edition.
evidence.summary: Confirms bibliographic identifiers and access-restricted-item=true; the page offers controlled borrowing and reports no suitable anonymous download files.
notes: The checksum is for the cached public metadata JSON, not the restricted book bitstream.

evidence.citation: Patrologiae cursus completus, Series Latina, vol. 32 (Paris: J.-P. Migne, 1857), cols. 1451-1474; Internet Archive item patrologiaecurs30migngoog.
evidence.summary: Provides the complete digitized PL 32 volume containing the older pseudo-Augustinian Latin text that Horstmann names and prints for comparison.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright (revised April 2026).
evidence.summary: Explains the maximum 95-year U.S. term for works already protected before 1978 and states that U.S.-published works before 1 January 1931 are currently public domain; supports the conservative U.S. duration assessment for the old editions.

open_questions.question: Who was Thomas N., and which Latin exemplar or textual branch did he translate?
next_steps: Review the complete 1984 Ayto-Barratt introduction and later Aelred scholarship for reassessment of the Thomas Netter proposal and Latin variants.
Keep the source relation at work-and-section level until a physical Latin exemplar is demonstrated.

open_questions.question: Was CME00003 keyed manually, OCR-derived, or produced by a mixed workflow, and what correction level did U-M apply before the 2016 review?
next_steps: Request item-level production metadata or a raw distribution package from U-M if transcription accuracy becomes material.
Compare representative XML readings against both the BSB scan and Ayto's manuscript collation without assuming a production method.

open_questions.question: Is the 1984 EETS edition still in stock at the £30 / US$50 price printed in the Society's 2023 list?
next_steps: Verify current stock and price with Oxford University Press before purchase.
Retain ISBN 9780197222898 and the official list as stable discovery evidence even if price changes.

open_questions.question: What license, if any, governs redistribution of the local XML serialization and repository-specific modifications beyond U-M's No Copyright-United States assertion?
next_steps: Seek an explicit repository-level license or U-M statement covering downloadable source encodings.
Continue to distinguish the medieval text and public-domain old edition from XML markup and later repository changes.
